reviews.review_text: Stayed here a few times and have to admit it is one if not the best caravan park I've stayed at.Tracey,Peter,Cathy and the rest of the staff are friendly and helpful.always willing to help.ameneties and grounds are always clean and tidy,the park is walking distance to main street and Coles supermarket.only thing I can suggest is a camera at the off leash dog park as some people can't pick up after their dogs.(Don't know if it legal to install one)ive got 2 big dogs and never ever not picked up their poop.i will definitely stay here before any other park 😊😊

Friendly and helpful reception staff.  Amenities may be older but very clean.
Love that the park supplies a bath mat, that sure helps! Wish other parks would do this too?!

What a magic little park you wouldn’t want it any bigger . Walking distance to the Main Street of Bairnsdale not far from lakes entrance and eagle point . They have a beautiful pool and very clean . A pizza oven that we used . Management very friendly and very helpful. Nothing bad about this park at all and we will be back for sure . I was with a U3A group and we were very well looked after .
Very helpful and friendly staff, great location within walking distance to town
A great accomodation place dog friendly too

I stayed in a Double bed cabin with ensuite, it had everything I needed for the night whilst working in town. The staff were so friendly on check-in and very helpful with maps and keys. Definitely will stay again and maybe test the pool out next time.

Small camping/camper location but amazing facilities. Amazing baby/family room with bath and amenities, proper reception area, bbq, waterfront, pool, 10 mins walk to big supermarket, nice town. Really great value.
Small park but great place to stay. Everything is super clean and well maintained.
Great place to stay highly recommend
Great street park with quarter pipe and bowl too. Really nicely spread out with lots of space. We'll be back to play!

I don’t like leaving negative reviews, but unfortunately my stay was quite disappointing. The cabin had a very strong smell of cigarette smoke when I arrived. Wasn't able to air it out properly as the fly screens were ripped, which meant I couldn’t leave the windows open without letting bugs in.

Cleanliness was also an issue. There was a dead cockroach stuck in the rangehood, and I found mouldy bread and leftover condiments in one of the cupboards from a previous guest... It’s hard to understand how these things were missed during cleaning?

The amenities block wasn't much better. It had a strong unpleasant smell, one of the shower drains was blocked, and the hand dryer was not working.

While I understand that facilities at a caravan park may be older or a bit dated, there’s no reason they shouldn’t be properly cleaned and maintained!
Excellent position on the Mitchell River with a walking track and easy walk to the CBD.

Great location, playground and pool for the kids with lots of green space. Walk to the shops easy enough and friendly operators. Will stay again one day.

Clean toliet and shower facilities  as well.
Great location, friendly staff, and value for money.  Our cabin could have done with a makeover but, it was clean and comfortable.
One night stay on a powered Van site , Amenities are clean , Site are a bit tight and the River Walk did bring some noise through the night,
Enjoyed our stay here for 3 nights. But noisy at night with the traffic from the main road and very noisy Corinella. Facilities aged but clean. Nice jetty area to fish from at the park.

Friendly helpful staff.
Had a good stay.
The amenities were clean and the pool was clean and well managed. Although the wifi was practically nonexistent despite saying they had wifi

Excellent service from Tracey at reception. Other CPs could learn a thing or two from her. Friendly, helpful, timely responses. Efficient and knowledgeable.
Yes, the park is on the main road  but the upside is its a short walk to shops, cafes, galleries and regional services. The noise is a factor, and recommend sites nearer the TAFE if you have a choice. Close to bike trails and walking tracks..

Was a disappointing stay. We were positioned under the trees, next to a caravan that a person had left their dog inside. It barked and whined all night as the owner was elsewhere. Next morning another tourist said it had been in distress for 48 hours. The sites under the trees are very confined and we unfortunately were a major target of the 100+ cockatoos that crapped all over our car and van. We had thought it would be a nice location by the river but on the walk past the park you could barely see the river through the bush. Also the sullage points were away from the taps and hard to locate. Could do with some signage to inform guests of the sullage location.
Great little park right in the heart of town. Walking distance to supermarket. On the edge of the river & on the cycling rail trail. Helpful staff & nice pool.

Great place
Friendly staff
Beautiful grounds well kept
Very clean amenities even bath mats for shower area

Lovely lovely lovely,
Very accommodating. Great Park.
Good town to stock up before heading up the Princes Highway.

We recently spent the weekend with friends at this caravan park and would happily recommend it.  We got off to a bit of rocky start with incorrect dates booked but this was quickly rectified and we were able to check in and out on our preferred dates.  The staff were all friendly and courteous, they had great events and additional activities for the long weekend and the amenities whilst old, were clean and well maintained.  We spent a lovely few hours in the pool but also loved the big established trees that helped us keep cool.

Always a pleasurable stay at NRMA Bairnsdale with Managers Pete and Tracey. They are the most informative hosts with many activities for the kids dotted around the park. Music entertainment and food vans over the busy times. Always a smile and a friendly hello. Support staff are great wirh special mention for Cathy, amenities are very clean. The park has a well polished finish to it. Pete is always doing something.
Our third stay there and will stay again.
Close walk to Bairnsdale CBD for everything you might need. Short drive to popular beach resorts and great bike riding tracks.

Selected an un powered site, camp kitchen has all you need even if you don't have an esky or cooking supplies. It has saucepans, hot plates, air fryer, microwave, 2 fridge's, toaster, kettles and more.

Only stayed one night to break up a trip and the staff where so accommodating and the facilities and park is lovely we will be coming back for a longer stay for sure - absolute bonus for kids is the pool and across the road a giant all abilities playground - thanks for having us
We were only there for a short stay but Peter and Tracey were extremely helpful ,great facilities and the pool was awesome would definitely stay again 👍

We had to spend the night somewhere away from the bushfires and these guys were so accommodating for our motorcycle tour company! Very friendly, lovely green grass, great pool and great location! Thanks guys

Nice caravan park on the banks of the Mitchell River
Good sized sites many of which are shady
There is a fire pit and a pizza oven in the BBQ area
Amenities are clean and the entire place is tidy

Beautiful clean public showers. Beautiful pool area. Lovely family friendly park. Gets quiet packed over the holiday season . We took caravan we always book here when coming to bairnsdale . Friendly staff . Just awsome spot to spend the holidays with the family .
Close to river to fish and town
We stayed on a powered site for 2 nights and it was great 👍 clean amenities friendly staff and right beside the river and a close walk to town 👌 we will be back for sure

We stayed at the park on 5-8th Dec
Certainly not the biggest park but definitely up there as one of the best that l have stayed at
It caters  for everyone from couples to families
It has everything you could ask for when travelling in your van even down to
a amazing area to sit and have a glass of wine waiting for your pizza that you have made to cook in the park pizza oven
A big thank you to Ben in reception his help and knowledge of the area were fantastic
This caravan park could certainly teach bigger and overpriced caravan parks how it’s done
Wendy
Nice camping area and well maintained
Great place to stay with children. Great pool, Close to everything you need.  Awesome coffee machine in the laundry 👌

Ik verbleef 2 nachten in een cabin echt perfect . Alles was schoon en netjes. Toilet gebouw dicht in de buurt. De cabin heeft een perfecte kitchenette. Het park is netjes en verzorgd. Sanitair is schoon en netjes
Great 👍
The people were helpful and easygoing and I enjoyed the social interactions and the atmosphere.
Good
I was worried it would be noisy. The park looked full and it was next to the highway, but, it was so peaceful, clean and well equipped. Thanks Tracey for looking after us

The best holiday park in East Gippsland! Long weekends and holiday periods expect food trucks on site, live music, plus swimming pool, outdoor large screen tv, kids activities, and delightful park managers!

We have just had a truly lovely 3 night stay with Peter, Tracy and the team at this holiday park. The park is clean, well maintained and has fantastic amenities. The showers are amazing and we loved the extra thoughtfulness of providing bath mats for guests. Travelling with 4 kids between the two families, the park had thought of everything for the kids, with games on the lawn, movies on a big screen tv and the family friendly atmosphere of providing a beautiful communal fire pit and wood fired pizza oven, pre heated on request by the owner! Not to mention the pool with plenty of poolside lounges in the shade for the grown ups!
Location is amazing, there is a fishing jetty within the park, and walking distance to all the shops.
Very reasonable price for the service and amenities received! Highly highly recommend!

Set right on the beautiful banks of the Mitchell River, this park delivers relaxation with a view. We loved the easy access to the river-walk and cycle track, perfect for stretching the legs and soaking up the scenery. Tracey and Peter were wonderfully welcoming hosts, making us feel right at home from the moment we checked in. The facilities tick all the important boxes: swimming pool for a refreshing dip, well-maintained BBQ areas and camp kitchen, a handy laundry, and even jetty access to embrace riverside life. The grounds are magnificently maintained (particularly the fairy lights on the huge tree near reception). The amenities block was impressively clean, and having a bath mat provided was such a thoughtful touch. Although the park sits on a main road, we were pleasantly surprised that traffic noise wasn’t an issue at all. It still felt peaceful and relaxing, especially by the river.
All up, a friendly and comfortable stay in a lovely location. We’d happily return for another riverside getaway!

Set right on the beautiful banks of the Mitchell River, this park delivers relaxation with a view. We loved the easy access to the river-walk and cycle track, perfect for stretching the legs and soaking up the scenery.|Tracey and Peter were wonderfully welcoming hosts, making us feel right at home from the moment we checked in. The facilities tick all the important boxes: swimming pool for a refreshing dip, well-maintained BBQ areas and camp kitchen, a handy laundry, and even jetty access to embrace riverside life. The grounds are magnificently maintained (particularly the fairy lights in the huge tree near reception). The amenities block was impressively clean, and having a bath mat provided was such a thoughtful touch.|Although the park sits on a main road, we were pleasantly surprised that traffic noise wasn’t an issue at all. It still felt peaceful and relaxing, especially by the river.|All up, a friendly and comfortable stay in a lovely location. We’d happily return for another riverside getaway!
Only drawback is closeness to main road. However, it did get quiet late at night.

We had a stop over here in our camper for 1 night and loved it. Booked a powered site, and they put us right near the kids swing set which was perfect as we are travelling with our 2yo granddaughter.  People are friendly and welcoming, facilities are amazing, very clean, even had a bath for the little one ❤️
Highly recommend
Very good,  calm and quiet place 😘😘😘
the people here are so nice its such a lovley caravan park. everyone one is so nice and it feels so welcoming and i highly recommend coming here.

We stayed here for just one night. They have a excellent dog run yard with toys. There are bath mats in the amenities, that for me is 5 star.

The park is lovely kept with a nice big fire pit for everyone to enjoy.
Bairnsdale's 1 and only caravan park. Plenty of shade and close to town and shops. Great Kids park across the Hwy. Clean and tidy. Motorcycle friendly
Very friendly

What a fabulous caravan park! With friendly staff, great amenities including a communal area with a fire pit, BBQ and pizza oven under Fairy lights. There's also a little dog play area which was fantastic. When its warm, they have a lovely pool too. The river frontage is also great for a nice long walk. You can also walk to town too. Will definitely be back!
Loved we could take our fur baby with us and stay in a cabin.

Yes, I booked the budget non en-suite cabin.  The bed was the worst I have ever experienced, the heat pump sounded like a washing machine and the sink was full of ants. No amount of budget is worth this.

Good location, next to a river walk and close to the supermarket and restaurants.

Stayed one night in the cabin after long day of driving. Bed is so soft that you can’t sleep on it and AC sounds like an aircraft engine!

Nice Basic park,(we had all van site)  excellent walking track along the river, dog friendly, small dog park,  walking distance to shops and restaurants,  nice garden area with seats and wood fired pizza oven , amenities very clean,  I thought being on the main road it would be noisy but it wasn't a problem and I had no complaints.
Smallish Caravan Park..just off the main road..clean tidy and very hot showers 🚿 with volume..so good..easy walk to shops..
Right at end of foodie of Main Street close to river and highway but quiet

The staff were friendly and helpful at check-in. The 2 Amenitie blocks are quite old but clean, they are well set up with a mop, blade and broom but the best was the shower mats that were provided. It felt wonderful stepping on a clean floor mat after a hot shower, the mats were daily washed. Shower tap might benefit from a new washer, it is hard to turn the dripping shower off. Nice laundry,  has a hot drink machine with freshly ground coffee that takes card. The washing lines could be nearer to the laundry facility, there is a fenced pet area next to Amenities block. The camp kitchen is in the camping area near the pool. In the evening the outside sitting area is lit up by a tree wrapped in fairy lights. Easy access to the river walk. The Park has everything you need.

Riverside holiday park had everything we needed, in a great location. The ensuite cabin we had was clean and a great size for my partner, our dog and I. The bunks in the cabin would make it a good option for a family too. There was a decent sized off lead area for us to take our dog before bed each night for toileting and a bit of play.

Nice  caravan  park  also  with  cabins, located  next  to  town,  river, park  etc.  Area  flat,  some  van  sites  grassed, gravel  and  concrete,  nice  swimming  pool, playground,  walking  tracks, bike  track. Amenities  oldish  but  clean  and  floor  towels  for  showers.  Staff  friendly  and  helpful. Short  drives to  local  areas  attractions,  large  shopping  district. Stayed  here  before  many  years  ago  and  will  stay  again  when  in  district.

The cabin was clean an comfortable. The kitchen an bathroom could do with some work. But they worked well.its not somewhere I'd stay for more than a night. That's not the park itself just the whole area. They had the heating on for us which was fantastic  in winter.good price for a clean an comfortable  night stop.

Booked the day of for late check in which we confirmed over the phone. When we arrived at 9pm there was no check in envelope for us (even though there were envelopes for others) and we couldn’t get into the campsite. No number to call and we ended up having to find a motel down the road late at night.

Editing to reply to the reply: the booking was made and paid for for 11 April 25 in my partners name. The booking was for late check in for a campsite. Couldn’t get the the campsite as couldn’t get through the boom gates without checkin info, furthermore wouldn’t have been able to use the amenities without that info as they’re locked. Couldn’t check in because there wasn’t an envelope outside reception as stated - despite there being other enveloped in others names. Raised the problem with reception the next day 12 April and they just said they didn’t know what went wrong and apologised.

Thank you for a wonderful couple of days you are all kind, friendly and helpful, I will be back again.
Dog friendly with a good walking track along the river.
We loved the hot hot showers. And being on the lovely Mitchell river walk is a bonus

My partner and I were traveling up the east coast when our ute needed major repairs. We called and made a last-minute booking to stay at the campground. Everyone who worked there was amazing and accommodating for us while we were stuck for an entire day without a vehicle. Would recommend as the people are very lovely and the place is kept extremely clean.

First camping trip at the Gippsland which we found ourselves a spot here for 4 nights.

Showers have strong water pressure and are hot. Bathmats were a plus. Toilets are cleaned daily. Would've been great if cleaning was done twice a day on peak season.

Every night or so there's activities organized at the common area, with a campfire. Food trucks do come and go through the day. It was nice that kids got to enjoy some Greek doughnuts. Common kitchen is little old but usable. Every morning I'd be woken up by the loud noises of the birds. It's also right next to a main road with noise from traffic, which winds down later in the evening.
It was nice to take a walk next to the Mitchell River. The Koala trail at Raymond Island was just a short drive from here.
Absolutely awesome staff, great location.
Can’t recommend Pete and Tracey enough! What an absolute pleasure our holidays have been, thanks for the memories.
Recently I stayed overnight. The CSO Ben was extremely helpful with local knowledge and Caravaning tips. Lovely loop walk from the cvp around Mitchell River and very close to all services.

6th time we have stayed here. Ideal position, easy walking distance to shops/supermarket. Good value with good sites for caravans/tents. Well maintained with clean facilities and a great swimming pool. Peter and Tracey are excellent hosts. Defintely recomend. Excellent Chinese Restaurant directly across the road.

Ja netter kleiner Campingplatz
Mit sehr freundlichem Empfang
Cabine gut ausgestattet

Park is beautifully maintained, gardens kept lovely. And staff were friendly and helpful. Only stayed one night but it was a little noisy from traffic on highway as we were close to the entrance.
River walking track is on the edge of the park with no fence so was a little concerned about security.
In saying all that, it’s a great stopover park when traveling and close to town.

Back to this caravan park again  , this time in a cabin with my dog. Only problem ( and it cannot be resolved) it the noise of vehicles,  particularly trucks , decelerating as the highway narrows to single lane, the traffic is bumper to bumper all day, surely whoever is responsible for this inane can do something about it.|

We had a great unpowered site by the pool. Great for a couple of nights, walk to shops and river. Amenities very clean and code entry. Friendly staff. Great place for a reset on a predominantly free camping tour of Aus🤠👍

Campground is overall good but unfortunately some of the facilities are not well maintained. The kitchen near us was disgusting - people living there permanently treating it as their private kitchen, leaving dishes everywhere and all surfaces dirty and it’s quite smelly too. Bathrooms were not very clean either. Lovely plus - they provide bath mats for your comfort. It’s so good to step into a clean bath mats after shower!
Great camp site but take ear plugs for the birds....ha ha.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
I stayed here for 2.5 months last year for work, and came back recently to see friends and stay here again. Tracey and Peter are fantastic, very friendly and kick ass at management. Peter’s help makes me believe I can reverse my caravan seamlessly.
The whole team of staff are lovely. The grounds are tidy and clean and have everything you need.

I was very pleasantly surprised by the friendly reception I received. Because I was camping with a motorcycle, I was even offered a loan-towel to avoid me having to pack a wet one. So thoughtful! Thanks.

We stayed here in our Caravan on Labor day long weekend, with 2 other groups we all had a great time staff were friendly. We also had our 2 dogs with us. We used the off leash area which worked well for us. Overall a great experience we will be back, as its a good base point to go out and explore.
Some of the campers were noisy and shouting up to 2am. The caretakers don't seem to care.

Been there many time
Friendly staff.

2025/03/11
テントサイト32$。少し高いが付近にテントokのキャンプ場が無いので仕方がない。プール、シャワー、Wi-Fi、共用キッチンなど設備は整っていて不自由ないのでホテルと思ったら安い。徒歩圏内にColesもある。オウムがうるさい。

Drove in, office staff lovely.  Went to our site and parked to the right of power box as indicated by arrow on the box.  Asked to move further left by office staff  no issue.  We were then approached by the RUDEST man and told to move on to our site.  When we questioned the arrow pointing to our site we were told who cares go to office and get a refund if you don’t like it!!! We certainly did!  Maybe a customer service course would be appropriate!! Not how you want to be treated after a long drive

Great value.
Service was quick and friendly
Definitely stay again

Stayed with as a group of 6 for a night and enjoyed ourselves. Access is a bit tricky crossing Prince’s Highway from the east but if traffic isn’t heavy you can make the turn relatively easily, though there was construction on the highway that lead to some backups near the turn.

Our holiday unit was clean and well maintained. We enjoyed the park’s pool and the riverfront location offered some nice scenery as well.

• Trees for shade
• Fully equipped kitchen in the middle of the campsites
• Very clean showers and toilets
• Close to the road, so it can be a bit noisy
• Small pool

I recommande this place, was nice!!

Friendly staff
Clean amenities
Lovely swimming pool
We had a powered site
Dog friendly
Great location, clean and tidy and convenient

This review pertains to my camping experience on Australia Day, 2025.  We reserved our campsites two weeks in advance, and they were nearly fully booked. We selected a powered site. As frequent campers, we usually choose NRMA due to their high quality and welcoming atmosphere. However, our experience at Bairnsdale was disappointing due to the assigned campsite's location. The site was intended for caravans, resulting in limited grassy areas for our tent and a very uncomfortable night's sleep.

Furthermore, while we were required to sign a declaration promising our dog would not bark, we were not informed about the potential noise from the resident Cockatoos. Despite our efforts to keep our dog quiet, a neighbouring tent with two barking and growling dogs disrupted our sleep.

While these may seem like minor issues, given NRMA's reputation and growing customer base, greater transparency regarding potential limitations would enhance the overall camping experience.

We had a fantastic holiday at NRMA Bairnsdale Riverside Holiday Park! The staff was welcoming and friendly, and the overall atmosphere was great. The only reason for 4 stars is that the kitchen could have been cleaner. Otherwise, a wonderful stay!

We had a great time staying here. The site was fine. Bathrooms got messy after the long weekend. Stalls were running out of paper. Soap dispenser was empty.

They hosted a free bbq which was so good. And a Mexican food truck another night was welcome.

this place was amazing they had a big pool and i had lots of fun in it and the staff are very nice and on my last day here they set up an outdoor cinema and we got popcorn. i loved how nice and relaxing this place is and i also like the river i went fishing in it but i caught nothing. overall great place to visit i recommend going to this place
Great place to leisure and fun. The river walk and Bristo in-house are added bonus. Kids also enjoyed the stay.

With Tracey and Peter welcoming us in for two Christmas Holidays in a row we can confidently say that this little park has so much to offer. With food and drink vans coming into the park every other day, live music and karaoke for the family, a sneaky icy pole on a hot day and even bath mats for the showers - they’ve thought of everything for a perfect family summer holiday.

Location wise you’ll find everything you need with a short 5 minute walk into Bairnsdale, 10-35 minute drive to Lakes Entrance for the beach, Paynesville for fishing or Koala sight seeing at Raymond Island, river swimming or even a game of Golf.

Sites are decently spaced for small and larger vans with some grassed, concrete or dirt.

Can’t wait to return Dec - Jan 2026

We absolutely loved our stay at NRMA Bairnsdale Riverside. We stayed on a powered grass site along the back fence. The whole park was shady and well maintained, it was peaceful and very relaxing. While the toilet blocks were a bit dated they were clean with nice hot showers and great water pressure. The pool was beautiful and the communal triangle area was absolutely superb. The kids had a blast with the outdoor tv on the tree and we made use of the outdoor games and pizza oven. The park is pet friendly, which we didn’t need but they had a fenced area for dogs to be off lead. Our son loved the jetty, so much so that we had to go out and buy fishing rods. Would highly recommend and definitely stay again. Our only negative was that there was a bit of highway noise as the fence we were on faced the main road, however other areas of the park did seem quieter.
Great leafy park close to town. I would recommend this park.
Well maintained, clean, simple, priced right. 4.5 stars
Cabin small but totally clean and comfortable. It was well equipped with everything we needed. Staff follow up was exceptional.

Great holiday over the weekend excellent friendly staff who organised awesome entertainment and food vans. The park and facilities were always clean.
Great location only a short walk to town centre.
Second time stay and many more to come.
Our favourite park and we have been to many around Australia, amazing staff, facilities are excellent, close to everything. We enjoy every stay very much. 10/10
Very friendly staff,noisy ,due to Pacific hwy going through town, walking distance to shops ,great river walk

Nice caravan park friendly caretakers so helpful nice layout close to shopping centre clean amenities great place in a good location with a nice team of staff highly recommended for a day or a extended stay
Lovely park and owners make you very welcome.  Only downside is it's not disabled friendly.

Easily one of the best caravan parks we’ve stayed in. Very clean and organised, friendly hosts and great amenities. Close to Coles and local shops. The most wonderful thing was that they provide clean bath mats in the showers! Such a luxury. Camp kitchen has great facilities and clean tea towels. Great value also at $29 for an unpowered site. Even had a fire pit going during the evening!

Our experience at Bairnsdale NRMA Riverside Holiday Park was nothing short of exceptional! We stayed on one of the powered grass sites, and it was the perfect setting for a relaxing camping trip. The park is wonderfully maintained, offering clean amenities and consistently hot showers. What we loved most was how accommodating the park is for everyone, including our furry family members—it's truly pet-friendly. Our kids had an absolute blast, and the atmosphere felt safe and welcoming.

The customer service here is top-notch. The staff were friendly, attentive, and went out of their way to ensure we had a pleasant stay. We felt like we had complete freedom to explore and enjoy all that the park has to offer. It’s the ideal place for a family getaway with its beautiful surroundings and well-kept facilities. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a relaxing and family-friendly spot to camp!
Too close to main road for us.
Great place to stay, very friendly and helpful staff,  thoroughly enjoyed our 2 night stay, will definitely go again.
Fantastic facilities and staff. Super clean and tidy.
Wonder caravan park. On the Mitchell  River. So peaceful and relaxing

Absolutely fantastic customer service! We stayed in one of the ensuite cabins, well stocked, clean and comfortable. Was nice to walk in to a warm cabin, its the little things like that that make the stay even better! We will definitely come back.

such a amazing place with the friendliest staff i’ve met!! i got given a upgrade to a lovely cabin with a ensuite and the girls also signed me up to their yearly membership :). Great family park with an awesome 6km loop around the river for those that enjoy a walk/run like myself. Would highly reccomend and can’t wait to stay again!
Older NRMA park but clean and tidy

This caravan park has the usual facilities.  The showers are old but are terrific with lots of hot water and good pressure.  They even provide bath mats, which is a bonus. Located walking distance to shops and close to the start of the rail trail.

We stayed in one of the larger cabins with two bedrooms, one with bunk beds (which we didn't use) and a double bed in the other. The cabin was slightly knocked around from standard wear and tear, but it was very comfortable and clean. The people running the caravan park were friendly and seemed to care about creating a nice environment. We were also able to take our two dogs and there was even a little fenced area for the dogs play in.
Kind and warm people, excellent service 🫶🏻

Nice place, staff were lovely, we had a laugh and a chat. We had a powered slab site opposite the river where there were so many carp ! Nice walking track too. Near the highway but didn't really bother us overnight in the caravan.We only stayed one night but I think it would have been nice to have stayed longer.

This is a wonderful park set right on the Mitchell River and quickly accessible to the 5km walk along the river. We stayed in one of the units with our dog and it was perfect! Clean, neat and cosy. Staff were wonderful and great with communication. See you again!!

This park is really special. So many extra little touches make it a wonderful place to stay. The park itself is beautiful, with lots of mature trees, and generous space for each van so that there isn't that 'carpark' vibe. But it's the little extra touches that make it really special. Clean bathmats put out in the amenities blocks!!! Oh, the luxury of those bathmats! Anyone who has ever used public showers will relate to this. Clean and white and fresh every day. You take one from the pile and after your shower you drop it in the laundry bin.
The park is very dog-friendly too, and even provides a fenced off-lead exercise area. This isn't just a bit of ground - it's right in the middle of the park. And in it are provided a big bowl of water, kept clean and full, a bench with cushions for the humans' comfort, and even a well-equipped toy library!
If I had to stay in a caravan park again, this is definitely the one I'd choose.

Our cabin was lovely and clean with lots of space. As the cabin was near the river we didn’t experience any traffic noise. Tracey at reception was extremely friendly and welcoming.  A lovely spot near the river.
Stayed here for the weekend with a girlfriend. The place was awesome! Great amenities and fantastic staff, they were very helpful. Highly recommend staying here. Will definitely be back.

Managers Tracey and Peter were very friendly amenities were clean showers maintained temperature without going hot and cold bath mats supplied.close to shops and attractions own jetty for fishing in the river with plenty of things to keep family entertained even off leash dog area with toys. Nice place to visit and stay. Have mobile food van with many delights
The Caravan park is absolutely lovely, and amenities are very clean. Plenty of walking tracks and our little Jack Russel loves it too! Powered site. Thank you 😊
Stayed there in  cabin for four days. Totally with the money. Cabins are clean and toilets are also well maintained. Caravans are situated in convenient distance for everyplace.
Caretakers were very attentive to everyone, awesome communal space with Woodfire pizza oven, bathrooms very clean and comfortable, showers 10/10

It was a lovely experience staying at this caravan park. The kids had so much fun with the activities organized. All of us had a good time fishing at the dock nearby. The cockatoos are a nuisance but there isn't much one can do about it. Would love to come back again next year.
This is an awesome camp site. Very friendly reception and kids safe. Small but great site for family camping.
Fantastic place for holiday and fishing right at your doorstep

The toilet is very clean and there are no bugs! And there is a swimming pool and a lot of equipment, which is great. The only drawback is that there are a lot of parrots and they are very noisy. There will be hundreds of birds waking you up in the early morning! And there will be a lot of bird poop on your car roof and tent!

Bathmats for the showers are awesome, heaps of games to play and pool is great. Service was awesome, only gripe was the hundreds of cockatoo's which made it somewhat difficult to enjoy the serenity and a sleep in!
Excellent service and location

A great base from which to explore the region. Tracy and Peter went above and beyond to help after the recent storm, under somewhat trying circumstances.
Cannot fault the service and friendliness of all the staff. Highly recommend, from site 5.

We loved this caravan park, it's a great location right near the town and the amenities are clean and tidy.
The park was quiet with a bush like setting and the caravan sites are spacious. We would definitely visit again.

Are you after a cabin where:

1) Windows are so gummed up with dirt that even when you an opened them it doesn’t slide but grind?
2) A cabin perched directly under deciduous trees where a huge colony of cockatoos perch and are so noisey you cannot take a phone call with any of the windows open?
3) Or a front door that will not stay open when you want it too?
4) Or a shower so tiny that if you turn from front to back either side of your body bumps the taps so you can get either scalded or frozen….scalded for the most part…don’t even try to wash your hair!
5) A cabin close to the main highway where all night long traffic of huge semi trailers regales you with compression brakes and horns
6) A cabin, that even if you get the front door to stay open looks out to a multitude of caravans looking back at you; and
7) Where one cannot park one’s car next to the cabin but partially on and off the road and their gas provider truck nearly crashes into your car until you scream at  the driver to stop!
8) And….as a bonus, where the furniture is so uncomfortable you cannot sit for too long whilst you throw your neck out trying to watch their minuscule TV almost on the ceiling.

Well this accommodation is for you!

One of the worst of any we have ever stayed in! Never again!
Awesome spot, friendly grounds staff and we'll located!

Good facilities and effort by management, but let down by a few things:|- Terrible road noise. Camping is meant to be relaxing; trying to sleep beside a highway with no sound barrier is not. |- Tightly packed in. |- No entry / exit security gates. |- Wind easily cuts across the site|- Aside from a smaller jetty, the river frontage offers no benefit. Can't see river from site. ||While things like the camp kitchen; events; pool try to make it pleasant, that road noise and wind could really benefit from a solid fence put around the site on the highway side.
We enjoyed our stay 😊

Lovely, well maintained caravan park with plenty of shady spots and a nice pool. Friendly and conscientious staff who put on some activities for the kids. Exercise area for the dogs. Well positioned, close to good restaurants and supermarkets. Close to the start of the very scenic Bairnsdale to Orbost rail trail.

We had a a lovely stay at the park
Staff where friendly and accommodating
Very kid friendly with activities, great pool, nice river walk, with a park across the road also a skate park

Enjoyed our stay. Nice and close to river and shops. Managers friendly.
Clean and tidy park.

After having significant car trouble we rolled into the park at 1.30am with two cranky toddlers! The park seamlessly let us set up in a quiet area away from others and were very flexible with our unknown arrangements (due to car trouble). The owners were so great they didn't even charge us a fee for the night... great people!! We are super grateful for their help and hospitality! Highly recommend staying here!

We had a delightful stay at this caravan park – impeccably clean, well-organized, and surrounded by many activities to do around Bairnsdale. The owners (Peter) were incredibly friendly, and the ‘Gippy Goodies’ morning coffee and breakfast van (Neil) was the perfect start to each day. Highly recommended for a peaceful and enjoyable experience.

Très bon service et sanitaire très propre !
Nous avons beaucoup apprécié ce camping
Great park, lots of stuff out for the kids, great toilets and showers and super helpful at the front desk

Enjoyed our stay. Customer service is above and beyond. my husband is a double amputee and we had immediate assistance once they noticed. My family and I enjoyed our time we just wished there was a cabin that caters to someone with disabilities eg. (Wheelchair). The only other downfall was the advertisement of wifi but the wifi has no coverage at all within the area. Other then that our stay was pleasant 😃
Really close to the city centre
If you can manage the noise of the birds of an evening and early morning then the Camp provides a good base for exploring the region, just remember to bring some earplugs!

Unpowered site is next to the main street, so it was hard to sleep with street noises.
Staffs were friendly and amenities provided with cheap price of unpowered site though.

Caravan park disabled toilet unable to use two cubicles, one for toilet and one for shower only just bigger than standard cubicles
I have scooter and useless

Terrific caravan park in an ideal location. The customer service from Tracey and Peter are amazing - they are wonderful, kind and engaging, and also focused on optimising the customer experience. The facilities are well maintained as well. Overall, an excellent experience and we highly recommend this park to others.

Stayed for 29 days/ nights in a powered caravan site in my caravan.||Cannot complain at all.||Facilities always clean and maintained.||Peter and Tracey most welcoming, informative and most friendly. Nothing a bother to them.
Very tidy park, short stroll to the shops, amenities are very clean, sites are small but ample

Apprehensive about travelling 5 hours, with so many flood watches around, we decided to still go, and we are so glad we did! We had the best time at NRMA Bairnsdale!
The amenities were in an older building, but the cleanest and best kept I've seen at a holiday park! The bath mats were an incredibly luxurious touch when camping too!
The pouring rain didn't dampen our fun, as we sat in the camp kitchen chatting till the early hours of the morning, without bothering fellow campers! It was ace! We were stoked to still use the awesome wood fire pizza oven, even though it was pouring rain!
Thanks so much Peter and Tracey for being so welcoming! We can't wait to come back and enjoy your park again! Hopefully in much better weather so we can enjoy the pool and fire more!
Super friendly and helpful staff. Very clean amenities with wonderful hot showers!  Proximity to everything you could need a big plus.

A great holiday destination for a family holiday, dog friendly, walking distance from shops and a well maintained walking track along the Mitchell River. I recommend adding this to your holiday destination!

Fantastic place to visit, the staff are friendly

The amenities are spotlessly clean.

Lots of activities for the kids to do and the park is pet friendly

Highly recommend 😀

Stayed one night on our road trip. Very welcoming. Loved the complimentary bath mat. While the amenities block was 70’s retro is was spotless. Walk to everything. The only negative was the noise of the trucks going past.

I felt very comfortable and welcome here,
The staff were happy to help in any way possible to make my stay here comfortable.
Can’t go wrong staying here. It’s our regular go to when we’re in Bairnsdale. Service is 10/10, location is great and clean amenities. Highly recommended!

My husband and I came to the holiday park basically for a shower and a sleep on our way through. Cost us $73 for a cabin with our nrma road side assistance. We came after hours, the lock box process was seamless and well communicated. The cabin was clean and cozy. Air con/heater, a full kitchen, fresh bedding, towels and soaps - even had tea and a Tim tam waiting for us! Bath mats provided in the very well kept communal bathrooms and a lovely hot shower. The bed was comfortable but a little bit sunken in, it just meant we had to cuddle all night which we didn’t mind haha! Thanks and hats off to the great team who run this place!

Staff were friendly and welcoming.
Cabin had everything we needed and was clean and comfortable.
Grounds were tidy and well maintained. Thank you for accommodating us and our pets.

Great place to stay. To cheap for a cabin could easily charge more. Good friendly staff or owners. Best place I've stayed of the 13 hotels I've had of my trip.
Motorcycle friendly.
Good Accomodation

Amazing hospitality from the managers Tracey and Peter. We were late arrivals and they had a gorgeous fire pit going to sit around. and served us a hot meal straight from their pizza oven. Above and beyond  👏🏼👏🏼👏🏼
Super great spot to stop on your way or stay for a while. We checked in late and the welcome pack was waiting for us thanks So much.

Awesome park, free live music and a sausage sizzle on tonight!
Best amenities ever! They were so clean and smelt wonderful even at 6pm!
The bath mats added a little luxury to the whole experience! If you’re passing through the area absolutely stop here!
Lovely place to stop
Lovely welcome very clean amenities.

Come set up a tent at the unpower site, price 32 dollars for  2 people, clean bathroom and kitchen.  Reception staff give good advice.  It is next to the canal and road so you can hear cars driving by.There are many people staying, not lonely.

The staff here were so friendly and helpful.
This is the cleanest place I have stayed in, no grubby remote controls or dirty glasses at the back of the cupboard.

Trev and I are currently staying in Bairnsdale Caravan park, which is one of the best kept Caravan parks we have seen on our travels
Everything from Amenities, to camp kitchen, herb gardens, common gardens are in tip top condition.
A real credit to the managers of this park
We requested a site without a tree, staff could not of been more helpful
Will be back again.
It’s centrally located, close to everything, not too noisy from street traffic
Great Park friendly staff great amenities very clean & tidy. Highly recommend 👌

Adam and the team just made my stay so easy and care free!! The cabins were clean and cosy and they left the heater on for me as I arrived late. The extra touch of the lovely text message at the end of my stay wishing me safe travels was so considerate. Thank you for making my stay fantastic. Highly recommended

We had to cancel our booking at short notice and change to another NRMA park in Echuca. The staff were very friendly and nothing was too much trouble. We certainly recommend Bairnsdale Holiday Park and look forward to staying another time.

Great quuet park on the banks of the Mitchell River. Clean amenities, good hot water and providing a bath mat is a real plus and definately appreciated. No road noise, beautifully treed. Main street shops and large supermarket a very easy walk. Mitchell River Hotel plus other restaurants closeby. Lady in office very pleasant and helpful.
Especially appreciated the heater being turned on previous to my arrival as I had to walk from the station and it was a pretty cold night.
Amazing.. just come and do your finishing next to your place
Great park .very well maintained. Great friendly helpful Staff , great friday night free 🌭 & great entertainment. Under the tree canopy. Thank you Adam for making us so welcome .we will be back .

Great location with Riverside walk very enjoyable late afternoon. They had the heating on prior to our arrival and it beautifully warm and cosy when we arrived. Were very helpful in sorting out a "hiccup" with the online booking and did so graciously and to our complete satisfaction. Lovely area to visit. Highly recommend the team at this caravan park and the accommodation.

Julie and Carole, Canberra
Very ordinary needs TLC
Spacious, clean, comfortable 👌

Our stay was so pleasant! We stayed one night and arrived after hours but we were provided clear information on how to get our key and on a freezing night opened our cabin to a split system heater running! This so lovely to feel welcomed and thought about.
Staff were absolutely lovely and welcoming. We received a caring letter on the kitchen bench along with a welcome text and goodbye text the following day.
Location is beautiful and price was fair.
We will sure be back thanks to the staff and them going above and beyond! Thank you Adam and the team.
